_meta.author: Claude scorecard-refine-local autopilot
_meta.note: GA local batch 4: Atlanta D11 (Wayne Martin, new Jan 2026) + Savannah council (Blakely, Lanier, Bell, Leggett). Rosters web-verified current June 2026. Evidence-only: every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence; unanimous-vote items scored only where the member's seating at the time is confirmed; ambiguous procedural items (Oct 2024 Pride proclamation agenda vote) left null. Savannah offices restyled 'City Council Alderman/Alderwoman ...' so classify_office_tier resolves local (bare 'Alderman' falls through to state — systemic classifier gap logged in OVERNIGHT-PROGRESS.md).

profile.background: VP of Government Affairs, Morehouse School of Medicine; former executive director, Atlanta Board of Education; Atlanta Housing Authority and council chief-of-staff background; president/CEO of Covenant Ministry, a medical charity of Cathedral of Faith Church

wayne-martin.notes_set: [2026-06-09 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: won the Dec 2, 2025 runoff 58-42 over Nate Jester for the D11 seat vacated by Marci Collier Overstreet (elected Council President); sworn Jan 5, 2026; term to Jan 2030 (citycouncil.atlantaga.gov; Atlanta Civic Circle 12/3/2025). Nonpartisan office; no party affiliation documented; endorsed by Mayor Andre Dickens, Overstreet, and Andrew Young. Ordained minister (Rough Draft Atlanta 4/28/2025). THIN RECORD by design - ~5 months in office, took office after the FY2026 budget was adopted; no roll-call or social-issue record found yet. Only scored cell: campaign-site public-safety page touts coordinating with APD to put newly graduated officers on foot/bike patrols in D11 - affirmative pro-police advocacy (public_justice q0 TRUE). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, marriage/gender/Pride, schools, Christian liberty policy, fiscal votes, elections, the public-safety training center, state preemption, immigration, or guns - all null, not penalized. Headline % rests on a single cell; re-score after his first budget cycle.

0.note: Campaign public-safety page: coordinated with APD to place newly graduated officers on foot and bike patrols in District 11; frames safety as presence, prevention, accountability. Affirmative advocacy, not a mere budget vote.

profile.background: U.S. Air Force veteran; former ILA Local 1414 longshoreman; founder of the Bullhorn Crew anti-gun-violence group; Savannah State alumna; first Black woman to hold At-Large Post 2

alicia-miller-blakely.notes_set: [2026-06-09 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Post 2, re-elected Nov 2023, term 2024-2027 (savannahga.gov/3026; Jan 2026 action minutes). HARD evidence: seated member when council UNANIMOUSLY adopted the July 2022 resolution supporting 'women's rights to end a pregnancy' (WTOC) - sanctity_of_life q0/q1 FALSE. DEVIATION ALERT: voted NO on the 2022 gender-neutral bathroom plumbing-code change, own words: 'I surely don't want any of my granddaughters going into a stall where a male can walk in' (WTOC 5/2022) - biblical_marriage q2 TRUE. Voted NO on the 2022 millage, demanding the full rollback rate: 'I do not support this' (Fox28/WTOC 7/2022) - economic q2 TRUE. Seated for the unanimous April 2024 guns-in-unlocked-cars ordinance ($1,000/30-day penalties; struck down Nov 2025 as state-preempted) - self_defense q1 FALSE. Critical of SPD leadership (alleged corruption, WSAV) but no defund advocacy and no affirmative pro-police advocacy found - public_justice null. Oct 2024 Pride Month proclamation was mayor-presented; her individual role unverified - null. CHARACTER FLAG: shared a post calling the assassinated Charlie Kirk 'a racist,' captioned 'Tell it!!! We know who that poor excuse of a human being was!!' and faced removal calls (WTOC/WSAV 9/2025). NO EVIDENCE on schools, elections, immigration.

0.note: Seated member when council unanimously adopted a July 2022 resolution supporting women's rights to end a pregnancy - contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Unanimous council resolution supporting abortion access, July 2022 - documented contrary position to abolition.

2.note: Voted NO on the gender-neutral bathroom plumbing-code change, May 2022; own words: 'This is not something I would ever ever agree to... I surely don't want any of my granddaughters going into a stall where a male can walk in.'

Voted NO (6-3) on the 2022 millage of 12.20, advocating the full rollback rate of 11.399: 'I would prefer to vote for a rollback... I do not support this.' Also opposed TSPLOST over unclear community benefit.

1.note: Seated for the unanimous April 2024 ordinance penalizing guns left in unlocked vehicles ($1,000/30 days) - a local gun-control ordinance later struck down as preempted by Georgia law.

profile.background: 4th-generation Hudson Hill (West Savannah) resident; longshorewoman, ILA Local 1414; former police dispatcher and budget analyst; founder/CEO Ivory Bay CDC; MPA, Savannah State

bernetta-lanier.notes_set: [2026-06-09 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: District 1, current June 2026 (savannahga.gov/3028; April 2026 action minutes). HARD evidence: one of 3 NO votes on the 2022 millage REDUCTION (12.739 to 12.2 mills) - voted to keep the higher tax rate (Fox28 7/2022) - economic q2 FALSE (mixed record: sole NO on the MLK Arena demolition resolution and opposed the $8M Enmarket Arena parking contract, but those fights were over broken SPLOST promises to District 1, not spending restraint). Crime-as-societal-problem framing in her own words: 'We cannot police our way out of this situation'; gun violence is 'not so much a police problem but a societal problem' (WTOC 2/2024; she lost her own child to gun violence) - public_justice q3 FALSE; also publicly alleged corruption by SPD top brass (WSAV 5/2026) - no affirmative pro-police advocacy, q0 null. Voted YES (unanimous) on the April 2024 guns-in-unlocked-cars ordinance, struck down Nov 2025 as state-preempted - self_defense q1 FALSE. Oct 2024 Pride proclamation was body-level/mayor-presented; her individual role unverified - null. Westside environmental-justice advocacy noted but not a rubric cell. NO EVIDENCE on abortion, marriage, schools, Christian liberty, elections, immigration.

2.note: One of 3 NO votes on the 2022 millage reduction from 12.739 to 12.2 mills - voted against lowering the property-tax rate. (Joined the unanimous 2024 millage; opposed the $8M arena parking contract, but on community-benefit grounds.)

3.note: Own words after deadly weekend shootings, Feb 2024: 'We cannot police our way out of this situation'; gun violence is 'not so much a police problem but a societal problem,' urging cultural/societal investments - the societal/public-health framing this cell scores against.

1.note: Voted YES (unanimous) on the April 2024 ordinance penalizing guns left in unlocked vehicles (up to $1,000/30 days); struck down Nov 2025 as preempted by Georgia law.

profile.education: B.S. Mathematics, Shaw University; MPA, University of Georgia; M.Div & Christian Education, Shaw University
profile.background: Commonly 'Carol Bell.' 38-year City of Savannah career, retired Central Services Director (first Black female manager at the city, 1976); At-Large Post 1 2012-2019 incl. Mayor Pro-Tem 2016-2019; lost 2019; reclaimed the seat Nov 2023 with 51.4%

carolyn-bell.notes_set: [2026-06-09 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: Carolyn H. (Carol) Bell holds At-Large Post 1, term Jan 2024-Dec 2027 (savannahga.gov/3027; Fox28/SMN Nov 2023 result) - record office updated from generic At-Large. NOT on council for the July 2022 abortion resolution or the 2022 bathroom-code vote (out of office 2020-2023) - those cells null for her. HARD evidence: own words advocating a tax freeze - 'High taxes can really cripple our citizens,' prefers tourist/lodging revenue (The Savannahian 10/2023); seated for the unanimous 2024 millage and the 2025 ROLLBACK millage of 11.749, lowest since 1987 (WTOC 7/2025) - economic q2 TRUE. Affirmative pro-police advocacy in her own words: prioritizes public safety, wants a FULLY STAFFED police department - 'People need to feel safe. It doesn't matter what the stats say if people feel otherwise' (The Savannahian 10/2023) - public_justice q0 TRUE. Seated for the unanimous April 2024 guns-in-unlocked-cars ordinance (struck down Nov 2025 as state-preempted) - self_defense q1 FALSE. Licensed minister with an M.Div (electcarolbell.com) - biographical, not scored. ABSENT for the Oct 2024 Pride proclamation item (8-0-1) - null. NO EVIDENCE on abortion, marriage/gender, schools, Christian-liberty policy, elections, immigration.

2.note: Own words (10/2023): wants 'an overall freeze' on local citizen taxes - 'High taxes can really cripple our citizens'; seated for the 2025 rollback millage of 11.749 mills, the city's lowest since 1987.

0.note: Affirmative advocacy in her own words: prioritizes public safety and a fully staffed police department - 'People need to feel safe. It doesn't matter what the stats say if people feel otherwise.' No defund advocacy found.

1.note: Seated for the unanimous April 2024 ordinance penalizing guns left in unlocked vehicles - a local gun-control ordinance later struck down as preempted by Georgia law.

profile.religion: Lutheran (ordained deacon, Holy Spirit Lutheran Church, 35-year member)
profile.background: 25-year operating-room employee, Memorial Health; chairs the Chatham County jail Citizens Advisory Board; active in Savannah's End Gun Violence program; youth football coach

detric-leggett.notes_set: [2026-06-09 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified: District 2, 2nd term Jan 2024-Dec 2027, current June 2026 (savannahga.gov/3029; April 2026 minutes; WTOC 4/22/2026). HARD evidence: introduced Pastor Thomas Sills (Connor's Temple Baptist) to deliver the council invocation, 3/10/2022 minutes - affirmative support for prayer in a public body, christian_liberty q3 TRUE; ordained deacon at Holy Spirit Lutheran (Savannah Tribune 2014, city bio). Pro-enforcement in his own words after the Ellis Square mass shooting: 'we will not tolerate people being lawless' (WTOC 5/2024) - public_justice q0 TRUE; FLAG: Jan 2025 bodycam captured him accusing responding officers of aggression and suggesting an officer planted shell casings (WTOC 1/2025) - personal conflict, not a policy position; noted, not scored. Affirmed local decision-making during the SB 204 preemption fight: 'We can only just put forth what works for us locally... We just can't sit on our thumbs' (WTOC 5/13/2026) - refuse_state_overreach q3 TRUE. 'ICE is not welcome here in Chatham county,' DHS/ICE must 'check in' with the city, at the Forsyth Park protest after Dr. Linda Davis's ICE-involved death (The Savannahian 2/23/2026) - border q2 FALSE. Defended the gun-storage ordinance in his own words: 'We do support your right to have a weapon, but we don't support you when you are creating a space for somebody to take your weapon' (WTOC 5/2026) - self_defense q1 FALSE. Oct 2024 Pride proclamation: 8-0-1 agenda-amendment vote, mayor-presented, he did not speak - procedurally ambiguous, left null. Residency dispute raised by residents (WSAV) - noted only. NO EVIDENCE on abortion, marriage, schools, elections; 2025 rollback millage vote not broken out by member - null.

3.note: Personally introduced Pastor Thomas Sills of Connor's Temple Baptist Church to deliver the council invocation (3/10/2022 minutes) - affirmative participation in prayer in a public body; also an ordained church deacon.

0.note: Public pro-enforcement advocacy after the May 2024 Ellis Square mass shooting: 'we will not tolerate people being lawless,' backing SPD's accountability response; chairs the county jail Citizens Advisory Board. (Jan 2025 bodycam conflict with officers noted in record notes as a flag, not a policy position.)

3.note: Affirmed local-level decision-making during the state preemption fight: 'We can only just put forth what works for us locally... We just can't sit on our thumbs.'

2.note: At the Feb 23, 2026 Forsyth Park protest: 'ICE is not welcome here in Chatham county'; said DHS/ICE must 'check in' with the city - sanctuary-aligned stance, contrary to opposing sanctuary policies.

1.note: Defends the 2024 guns-in-unlocked-cars ordinance in his own words: 'We do support your right to have a weapon, but we don't support you when you are creating a space for somebody to take your weapon' - support for a local gun-control ordinance struck down as state-preempted.
